params.name: SC Energy Storage Unit
params.description:
On Oct 7, 1971 AD, the world's first superconducting energy storage unit debuted.
A superconducting energy storage unit is a device that uses superconductors' ability to have zero resistance at low temperatures to store electromagnetic energy for a long time. In a typical coil, the heat produced by the conductor's resistance results in a constant loss of electrical energy. To minimize losses when storing electrical energy, the device uses a superconductor coil capable of producing zero resist …
